problem.title: Meeting Rooms II
problem.description: Given an array of meeting time intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of conference rooms required to hold all meetings simultaneously.
assumptions: Multiple meetings can run in parallel in different rooms.
A room is free as soon as its current meeting ends.
Minimize the peak concurrent meeting count.

example.explanation: At time 5: meetings [0,30] and [5,10] overlap — need 2 rooms. Use a min-heap of end times: for each new meeting (sorted by start), if the earliest-ending room is free (heap.peek() <= start), reuse it. Otherwise add a room.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Min-Heap of End Times [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Sort meetings by start time. Use a min-heap to track the end times of active meetings. For each meeting, if the heap's minimum end time is <= the current meeting's start, the earliest-ending room is free — pop it and push the new end time (reuse the room). Otherwise, push the new end time (add a room). The heap size at the end is the answer.
